raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 52 is designed to strengthen and clarify tribal consultation processes under the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA).",
    "AB 52 will ensure tribal governments are recognized as the primary authorities on their own cultural heritage.",
    "The bill will require lead agencies to meaningfully engage tribes early in the environmental review process.",
    "Gaps in implementation of previous legislation have weakened Tribal Cultural Resource (TCR) protections.",
    "Non-tribal consultants and archaeologists often override tribal input, leading to costly lawsuits and threats to sacred lands.",
    "AB 52 aims to incorporate tribal expertise and cultural knowledge into the CEQA process and decisions.",
    "The bill seeks to strengthen protocols for unexpected discoveries of TCRs during development.",
    "AB 52 affirms the inclusion of both federally recognized and non-federally recognized tribes in consultation processes.",
    "The bill aligns state practices more closely with federal protections regarding tribal consultation."
  ]
}
```
